name: how-to-be-like-walt
description: Pat Williams' deep-dive into the life, character, and leadership principles of Walt Disney — drawing on hundreds of interviews with people who knew Walt, from family members to Imagineers to business partners.
useCase: Understanding creative leadership, perseverance through failure, building a culture of excellence, risk-taking in business, and living a life of optimism and imagination.
knownRisks: The book is unabashedly positive about Walt — does not dwell on controversies or darker aspects of his personality
Pat Williams writes from a Christian perspective which colors some of the lessons
Some stories come from secondhand interviews and may contain the normal embellishments of memory
Walt was a heavy smoker (3 packs/day) which contributed to his early death — not a trait to emulate
